derivation: Fresh decompile_function + disassemble_function of 0x64f840 and its callee FUN_005d6ae0 (0x5d6ae0, world-axis rotation helper) this task (2026-07). All 8 core vectors below are bit-exact emulate_function results, not hand-derived: a fully-linked fake struct graph was seeded (component -> framework -> {maxAngleHolder, axisHolder, holder -> chassis body}) with an identity chassis rotation and local steer axis (1,0,0), so worldFront=(1,0,0) and forwardSpeed=dot(linVel,worldFront)=linVel.x exactly. wheelCount was seeded as 0 to take the JLE branch directly to RET (confirmed by disassembly: the per-wheel loop is skipped entirely when wheelCount<=0, and XMM0 -- which holds computedAngle from the immediately-preceding MOVSS [ESI+0x10],XMM0 -- is not touched again before RET), so the computedAngle register readback is bit-exact for the full formula (angle = maxAngle*steer, then *= (fullSpeedLimit/forwardSpeed)^2 when fullSpeedLimit<=forwardSpeed), not just a pointer-chase probe. All 8 emulate_function calls returned success/hit_return=true/final_pc=deadbeef. A 9th vector (knownDeviation below) demonstrates the retail binary reaches its 0/0 division path (fullSpeedLimit=0, forwardSpeed=0 both satisfy the <= gate) and returns NaN there, while the current HkVehicleSteering.cs port has an added 'forwardSpeed > 0' guard (see file comment) that skips the branch and returns a non-NaN identity value instead -- a genuine, intentional-looking deviation from bit-exact retail behavior for this degenerate input, not a bug in this task's emulation.

vectors.name: below-fsl-identity-positive-steer
vectors.notes: forwardSpeed (10) < fullSpeedLimit (15): binary gate false, no falloff. angle = maxAngle*steer exactly. Emulated XMM0=0x3e99999a.

vectors.name: above-fsl-quadratic-falloff-positive-steer
vectors.notes: forwardSpeed (30) > fullSpeedLimit (15): r=15/30=0.5, r^2=0.25 (QUADRATIC, confirmed by disassembly MULSS XMM0,XMM0 before the second MULSS). angle = 0.6*0.8*0.25. Emulated XMM0=0x3df5c290.

vectors.name: exact-boundary-speed-eq-fsl
vectors.notes: forwardSpeed == fullSpeedLimit exactly: binary gate is <= (inclusive), so the branch IS taken, but r=15/15=1.0 exactly and r^2=1.0 exactly -> identity, same result as the below-FSL branch would give. Confirms the boundary is inclusive without changing the value. Emulated XMM0=0x3ed70a3e.

vectors.name: zero-steer-above-fsl
vectors.notes: steerInput=0 zeroes the result regardless of the falloff branch being active. Emulated XMM0=0x0.

vectors.name: negative-steer-above-fsl-falloff-sign-preserved
vectors.notes: Same magnitude as vector 2 but negative steer: r^2 is always positive (squared), so the falloff scales magnitude without flipping sign -- sign follows steerInput*maxAngle only. Emulated XMM0=0xbdf5c290.

vectors.name: negative-forward-speed-reversing-identity
vectors.notes: Negative (reversing) forwardSpeed: binary gate 'fullSpeedLimit <= forwardSpeed' (15 <= -10) is false with ordinary signed float comparison, so no falloff -- same identity result as vector 1. Confirms the retail COMISS-based gate handles negative speed without special-casing, and the C# port's plain '<=' matches it. Emulated XMM0=0x3e99999a, XMM1 (forwardSpeed readback)=0xc1200000 (-10.0).

vectors.name: extreme-high-speed-tiny-quadratic-ratio
vectors.notes: Stress test for the squared falloff at a large speed/FSL ratio: r=15/1000=0.015, r^2=0.000225, angle=0.6*1*0.000225. Also uses a 3-wheel doesSteer mask ([false,true,true]) to exercise per-wheel indexing beyond the 2-wheel front-steer pattern used elsewhere. Emulated XMM0=0x390d8eca.

vectors.name: just-past-boundary-epsilon-above-fsl
vectors.notes: forwardSpeed is the smallest float32 increment above fullSpeedLimit (15.0 -> nextafter). Complements vector 3 (exact boundary, r=1 identity): here r is one ULP below 1.0, giving a result one ULP below vector 3's, confirming the transition is continuous and the '<=' gate does not introduce a discontinuity at the boundary. Emulated XMM0=0x3ed70a3c (vs vector 3's 0x3ed70a3e -- exactly one ULP apart).

knownDeviation.name: degenerate-zero-fsl-zero-speed-div0

retailExpected.notes: Retail binary: fullSpeedLimit <= forwardSpeed (0.0 <= 0.0) is TRUE, so the branch IS taken: r = 0.0/0.0 = NaN (hardware DIVSS default QNaN, bit pattern 0x7fc00000 -- confirmed by emulate_function register readback, NOT hand-derived: an independent Python/NumPy computation of the same 0.0/0.0 produced a different QNaN payload (0xffc00000, sign bit set), so the emulator's value is the one that reflects real x86 hardware semantics and is authoritative here), r^2*angle = NaN. computedAngle = NaN in the retail binary for this input.

portActual.notes: HkVehicleSteering.ComputeWheelAngles has an added 'forwardSpeed > 0f' guard (see HkVehicleSteering.cs XML doc: 'Extra guard forwardSpeed > 0 avoids div-by-zero on degenerate inputs') not present in the retail binary. For this input forwardSpeed>0f is false, so the port skips the falloff branch entirely and returns the plain maxAngle*steerInput identity value (0.3) instead of NaN.

knownDeviation.verdict: DEVIATION: confirmed by emulation, not fixed here per task scope. See SteeringOracleTests.KnownDeviation_ZeroFullSpeedLimitZeroForwardSpeed_PortDiffersFromRetail (marked [Ignore("unblocked by C-phase steering fix")]).
